firm.name: Quantum Partners
firm.oneLiner: Quantum Partners is an independent, owner-managed M&A advisory boutique in Munich that supports technology and growth companies with corporate finance, especially company sales, acquisitions, and growth capital.
fitNotes: Clear fit for an AI-native deal platform because they run repeatable sell-side, buy-side, and fundraising processes.
They explicitly mention due diligence, data rooms, buyer outreach, and negotiation, which are workflow-heavy steps that software can streamline.
Their focus on technology and growth companies suggests a team likely to value speed, structure, and better process visibility.
They already publish many completed transactions, so a platform that improves throughput and consistency could be relevant.

signals.title: Published article on buyer choice in company sales
signals.detail: Know-how article compares strategic buyers and financial investors in M&A.

signals.title: Published transaction references page
signals.detail: Reference list shows many completed deals, including recent ones dated 2024 and 2025.

signals.title: Global M&A network membership
signals.detail: Site states they are a member of the global M&A network and part of Cognos Partners.

signals.title: Hiring for M&A Director
signals.detail: Career section on the home page lists an open M&A Director role in Munich.

play.angle: Lead with the fact that Quantum Partners is actively hiring for an M&A Director in Munich, which is a clean why-now signal that they may be scaling execution capacity. Position Fintalo as a way to help a lean advisory team run more mandates without adding more email, Excel, and manual coordination. Keep the message practical and process focused, not generic AI hype, and avoid leading with broad platform claims or anything that sounds like a replacement of their judgment.

objections.objection: We already have a process for running deals, and our team is small enough to manage it.
objections.counter: That is exactly where Fintalo tends to help, by reducing the manual overhead around NDA workflows, buyer tracking, document release, and collaboration so the team can stay focused on advisory work.

objections.objection: We do not want to introduce another system into live mandates.
objections.counter: Understood, the goal is not a heavy implementation. We usually start with a short demo and map it to one live process, so you can judge whether it fits before any rollout.

objections.objection: Our work is confidential, so we are cautious about new software.
objections.counter: That is a fair concern. Fintalo is built for deal work with secure collaboration and controlled document access, so it is meant to reduce risk versus scattered email and file sharing.

openingEmail.subject: Quick idea for your M&A process
openingEmail.body:
Hi Quantum Partners team,

I noticed you are hiring for an M&A Director in Munich. That usually means more live mandates and more coordination to keep tight across buyers, documents, and diligence.

Fintalo helps advisory teams run that workflow in one place, instead of email, Excel, and scattered data rooms. If useful, I would be glad to show you a short demo and compare it against how you handle deals today.

Best,
openingEmail.groundedFact: I noticed you are hiring for an M&A Director in Munich.

components.label: Tech-maturity fit
components.rationale: runs data rooms (warm to the category)

components.label: Firm-size fit
components.rationale: ~16 people: the boutique sweet spot for Fintalo.

components.label: Service / sector fit
components.rationale: Core M&A / corporate-finance advisory: the bullseye.

components.label: Timeliness (why-now)
components.rationale: 4 live "why now" signal(s); 3 decision-maker(s) with a public footprint to reference

unverified: team_size_estimate, team page lists many named people but no explicit headcount for the whole firm beyond 12 corporate finance experts on the home page.
cities, only Munich is clearly stated as the firm's office city on the pages provided.
has_client_portal, no client login or portal mentioned.
tombstone_count, 210 is a stated transaction count, but the site also shows many individual references without a separate verified total for all deals.
people focus, only role and short bios are stated for some team members, deeper specialization is limited on the pages provided.
